user_message: Robert Foster at Quantum Analytics just sent this. Help me pull the context.

Just checking in on the territory planning proposal. Our Head of Digital is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
Date: 2024-02-08 15:19 EST
Subject: Re: territory planning Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with territory planning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-13 12:28 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Quantum Analytics territory planning

Just checking in on the territory planning proposal. Our Head of Digital is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Quantum Analytics: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-M trap (No native 're-emergence' pattern distinct from dark-outreach in skill set); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-M failure mode (No native 're-emergence' pattern distinct from dark-outreach in skill set)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-M -- No native 're-emergence' pattern distinct from dark-outreach in skill set
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
